svg.text: Each student starts out as a new recruit.
required points are shown at the very end of this handbook.
Each week students who have earned enough points for promotion will receive their new rank badge
and get to choose a prize from the prize drawer at the end of class!
from missions turned in on Sunday are processed after class and thus count towards the next week’s
Students who reach the first “officer” rank (2nd Lieutenant) will receive a special certificate.
Students who reach the first “general” rank (Brigadier General) will receive yet another special
Students who earn this rank have demonstrated an amazing level of hard work and dedication.
the last day of class, they will not only receive a very special framed certificate, but several other
Students who want to attain this rank will need to get serious about class right away
in order to earn the necessary 200 points.
This is a big deal for the kids, and any small prize donations you can make to the prize drawer would
Please no food or candy, though!
Sergeant Tank is an animated dog I created in PowerPoint that helps us work on our memory verse
He uses his tank (or other vehicles) and various weapons to destroy different words
from the verse so we have to recite it each time filling in the missing words from memory.
likely hear your child talking about Tank, so I want you to know who he is
Please note that this Army theme and point structure apply only to my class (5:00). I strongly urge
parents to do their best to stick to one service time if at all possible.
sporadically or frequently switch service times lose the continuity of having the same teacher each
week and being accustomed to the class procedures.
Consistency is a huge help for learning,
In second grade, students spend three academic quarters studying the gospel of John.
through this study is that your child will grow in both their knowledge and love for the incarnate Son
During the fourth academic quarter, we study the Armor of God from Ephesians 6, and we’ll
